value: コレナニ

value: 単位間の関係が皆目わからない

value: こんな単位ある。お菓子一箱とか、カワイイ
value_en: They had units like this? Like “one box of sweets” — kind of cute.

value: 見当もつかない
value_en: I can't even begin to guess.

value: 興味あるナマエ
value_en: Interesting name.

value: 単価でみたい。というか、どれくらいの物理的規模感のものが、いくらなのか、みてみたい。貴重感というか高級感というか。
value_en: I want to see it by unit price. Or rather, I want to know what kind of physical quantity this actually was and how much it cost. Like how rare or luxurious it was.

value: 分量すごい増えているのに、金額はあまり増えてない。毎年、単価ダダ下がり。
value_en: The quantity goes way up, but the amount of money hardly increases. The unit price is dropping like crazy every year.

value: 逆に、単価どんどん上がってる。米、すごい。
value_en: On the other hand, the unit price keeps going up. Rice is wild.

value: すごい量多い。同じ単位でも桁違いに
value_en: That's a huge amount. Even with the same unit, it's on a completely different scale.

value: すごい増え方
value_en: What a huge jump.

value: いつ？めっちゃ前？
value_en: When? A really long time ago?

value: 町どうしの地理関係は？
value_en: What's the geographic relationship between these towns?

value: 町の規模感？ギュッとなってたら狭くても多い？
value_en: How big were the towns? If they were packed together, could they be populous even if small?

value: 同じ年にみんな「…」
value_en: Everyone has “...” in the same year.

value: 今の函館のどのあたりの地域なのか
value_en: Which area of present-day Hakodate would this be?

value: 産まれた？
value_en: Were they born?

value: 全人口比と全戸数比はほぼ同じ？
value_en: Is the share of total population almost the same as the share of total households?

value: 戸数の増減？引っ越し？家事？壊れた？
value_en: Change in number of households? Moving? Fires? Houses destroyed?

value: 全員知り合い？距離感どのくらい？
value_en: Did everyone know each other? How close-knit was it?

value: 読めない…
value_en: Can't read it...

value: 人口増えてるが全人口比減ってる→全人口が増加した
value_en: Population is increasing but its share of the total population is decreasing → the total population increased.

value: 男女比（男の方が多い）
value_en: Gender ratio (more men than women).

value: 今だったら怒られる
value_en: Youd get in trouble for this now.

value: 今もある弁天町と同じ…？
value_en: Is this the same as present-day Benten-cho...?

value: 大体4人家族
value_en: Roughly a family of four.

value: 年齢構成とか？？
value_en: Age structure or something??

value: 度数とは
value_en: What does “frequency” mean here?

value: この頃の12,789戸は全体あったうちのどのくらいの割合？何戸あったのだろう
value_en: Around this time, these 12,789 households—what percentage of the whole was that? How many households were there in total?

value: ショウワ9年の3月ここだけがとりあげられるのはすごい大火だ…
value_en: The fact that only March of Showa 9 gets singled out must mean it was a huge fire...

value: 1～5月のものは3月に焼けてしまったの？
value_en: Did the records for January to May get burned in March?

value: 全焼と半焼の概念分けだした基準とか
value_en: What was the criterion for separating total burn and partial burn?

value: ココの損害額はインパクトがあるこれがいわゆるハコダテ大火？
value_en: The damage amount here really stands out. Is this the famous Great Hakodate Fire?

value: 損害額8700円はよく考えるとちょっとだけ今だと
value_en: When you think about it, 8,700 yen in damages would only be a little today—

value: 多めの差は気になる
value_en: The fairly big difference stands out.

value: 死者数のカウントがないのは戸せきとかしっかりわかったなかったから？
value_en: Is there no death count because household registers etc. weren't firmly established yet?

value: 当時の円の価値からすると相当なダメージ
value_en: Given the value of the yen back then, that must have been massive damage.

value: 戦後はどのくらい建物あったのだろう
value_en: How many buildings were there after the war?

value: 件数とり災棟とり災件数の関係がわからない
value_en: I don't get the relationship between number of cases, affected buildings, and affected households.

value: 火事騒は非火災と言える？
value_en: Can a “fire scare” be considered a non-fire?

value: こんなに単位変わってるのに同じ数字を出されても100
value_en: Even though the units change this much, they still give the same number—100.

value: この中かっこは一体…？どういうイミ？
value_en: What on earth do these parentheses mean...?

value: そもそも毎年何件も全焼するくらいの火事あるのヤバい
value_en: More fundamentally, it's wild that there were enough fires every year for multiple total burnouts.

value: り災棟とり災世帯は重なりにはあるの？
value_en: Do the numbers for affected buildings and affected households overlap?

value: 消失面積に対する損害の額がちがう！
value_en: The amount of damage differs relative to the burned area!

value: 死者数意外と少なくない？
value_en: Aren't the death counts surprisingly low?

value: ↑住民の避難意識高いから？
value_en: ↑ Because residents were highly aware of evacuation?

value: 数年単位で単位変わっている？なぜ？
value_en: Are the units changing every few years? Why?

value: 表頭に「非住宅」があるのは大火のせい？それまではあんまなかったんだろうなあ
value_en: Is “non-residential” in the column header because of the great fire? Before that there probably wasn't much of it.

value: 大正5と大正10と棟数も坪数の差に比べて損害額（大正10）はすごい
value_en: Compared with Taisho 5 and Taisho 10, the damage amount in Taisho 10 is huge relative to the difference in building and tsubo counts.

value: 「火事騒」っていわゆるボヤ？なんかかわいい
value_en: “Fire scare” — does that mean what wed call a small fire? Kind of cute somehow.

value: 昭和16年までは損害額で昭和23からは損害見積額
value_en: Up to Showa 16 it says “amount of damage,” but from Showa 23 it becomes “estimated damage amount.”

value: 政党名とかの記述があるの昭和から（22とか）だ.イロイロあったし…
value_en: Party names start showing up from the Showa period (like 22). A lot had happened...

value: 投票率とかどんなもんだったのだろう…
value_en: I wonder what voter turnout was like...

value: キケンシャ数少ない,そもそも権利もってる人多くないのか
value_en: The number of eligible voters is small. Or maybe not many people had the right to vote in the first place?

value: 「イト」さんは女性？それまで全然いなかった
value_en: Was “Ito” a woman? There had been almost none before that.

value: 平出喜三郎,明治35～昭和5まで.けっこうつよい
value_en: Hiraide Kisaburo, from Meiji 35 to Showa 5 — pretty dominant.

value: 大正9まで区せんきょ,大正11から市せんきょ

value: イミフメイ
value_en: Makes no sense.

value: イミフメイ

value: 干菓子と水菓子の区別

value: 職業を女性だけ「女」と記されるのはなぜ？

value: 士→（農）→工→商の順？

value: 「肉」の意味は？

value: すごく数が少ない

value: 張替は大区に1人ずつ（たぶんもうからない）

value: 何順なんだろう

value: 濁酒ってなに

value: 偉い順？僧侶の順位がおもしろい

value: 2小区は、「下の職業」が多い

value: こっちは山というか陸地だ

value: 他とちがうにもほどがある

value: だいたい1世帯3～4人→核家族少ない？
value_en: Roughly 3–4 people per household → so not many nuclear families?

value: 明33年で人口増えすぎじゃない？
value_en: Isn't the population way too high in Meiji 33?

value: 新しい町なのに人口すごく多い
value_en: It's a new town, and yet the population is huge.

value: なぜか5.0でとういつ
value_en: Somehow it's standardized at 5.0.

value: 村は？
value_en: What about the villages?

value: この年人口すごくふえている
value_en: The population increases a lot in this year.

value: 意外と一戸平均あたりの人が少ない
value_en: Surprisingly few people per household on average.

value: 平均33ってありえるのか？
value_en: Is an average of 33 even possible?

value: すごく人口へっている
value_en: The population drops drastically.

value: 極端に1戸あたり人数が少ない2年つづけてるもたぶん正しい
value_en: The extremely low people-per-household figure continues for two years, but it's probably correct.

value: 行政が戸数や人口の数を把握する意味は分かるけど一戸平均を知る…はあったのだろうか
value_en: I understand why the administration would track household and population counts, but did they really care about average people per household...?

value: -は初年度でデータをとれなかったの意味？
value_en: Does “-” mean they couldn't collect data in the first year?

value: ときどき一戸平均が半分になる「戸」の数え方ちがう？

value: エクセルみたいに縦横の線を入れた方が見やすいかも
value_en: It might be easier to read if they added vertical and horizontal lines like in Excel.

value: この年は“－”がつかない
value_en: This year doesn't have a “-”.

value: データとれていないが、“…”の記号になっている（“－”）でない
value_en: The data isn't available, but it uses the symbol “...” instead of “-”.

value: 人口が多いと子供が多いというわけではなさそう
value_en: It doesn't seem like having a larger population necessarily means having more children.

value: この年だけ一戸平均少ない
value_en: Only this year has a low average per household.

value: 東川町だけ人が多すぎるので行政単位の作り方が？？っている

value: 異常値

value: これも異常値？

value: 戸数ふえたのに人口へっている

value: この年まで戸数、人口は微増

value: 異常値？

value: ここだけ一戸あたりの人の数の平均多くない？

value: 人口だけすごく減っている

value: ここだけ一戸平均少ない

value: へっている

value: 急増

value: へっている

value: なぜ減った？

value: 3倍

value: 紙の節約？

value: 第2次ベビーブームだから人数増えた？

value: 建物は1つ→校数
value_en: One building → number of schools.

value: 生まれたのはそれより 15年前: 12年前: 第二次ベビーブームの人たち
value_en: Those people would have been born 15 years earlier — or 12 years earlier: the second baby boom generation.

value: 私立中が1コなくなった！
value_en: One private junior high disappeared!

value: 国立中学があるからイナカじゃない感がある
value_en: Having a national junior high makes it feel less rural.

value: いあい、しらゆり、ラサールはまだ函大附？
value_en: Are Iai, Shirayuri, and La Salle still affiliated with Hakodai?

value: 新制とすべてついている
value_en: Everything is labeled “new system.”

value: 私立は女性教員が多い
value_en: Private schools have more female teachers.

value: 2校にしては少なすぎ？
value_en: Isn't that way too few for two schools?

value: 工業高校に流れた？農業に流れた？
value_en: Did they go to industrial high schools? Agricultural high schools?

value: s37-39多い 高校のやつと比べて... 女子はキープ男子は高校でへる なぜ？
value_en: S37–39 are high; compared with the high school data... girls stay steady, boys drop in high school — why?

value: 遺愛と白百合のみ！
value_en: Only Iai and Shirayuri!

value: なぞ
value_en: Mystery.

value: 激減
value_en: Dramatic drop.
